# AI Workflow Improvements

<!-- Generated by tools/ai-sync/scripts/render.py from tools/ai-sync/*.yaml and tools/ai-sync/templates/. Do not edit directly. -->

This document is the generated human-readable summary of approved durable workflow rules for the marketplace templates.

Consumer projects should capture raw execution notes in `.agent-skills/retro-notes.md`. To promote a durable rule upstream, update this marketplace repository's registry and rerender.

## Active Rules

| Rule ID | Category | Summary |
|---------|----------|---------|
| `AI-EXEC-001` | execution | Delegated implementer and reviewer agents must run autonomously. |
| `AI-EXEC-002` | execution | Spawn implementers matching peak parallelism once, then reuse them for later waves. |
| `AI-EXEC-003` | execution | Fetch and rebase onto main before spawning implementers. |
| `AI-EXEC-004` | execution | Worker prompts must limit each implementer to the one assigned task. |
| `AI-PLAN-001` | planning | Plans must pin exact shared type shapes before parallel work begins. |
| `AI-PLAN-002` | planning | Module scaffolding belongs to the earliest task that needs it for compilation. |
| `AI-PLAN-003` | planning | Plans must include export ownership when downstream tasks need a type across modules. |
| `AI-PLAN-004` | planning | Plans must account for warning cascades before removing blanket suppression attributes. |
| `AI-PLAN-005` | planning | Plans should not provide exact API pseudocode for alpha or unstable dependencies. |
| `AI-TEST-001` | testing | Test setup must clean every stateful backend used by the app. |
| `AI-TEST-002` | testing | Shared-backend integration tests must use serial execution when they mutate shared state. |
| `AI-TEST-003` | testing | When adding a config field that gates a new connection attempt, protect all construction call sites. |
| `AI-VERIFY-001` | verification | Same-compilation-unit parallel work may defer lint until the whole wave completes. |
| `AI-CLEANUP-001` | cleanup | Remove temporary dead-code suppressions after downstream consumers exist. |
| `AI-STATE-001` | state | Plans should prefer atomic operations over check-then-act patterns for shared mutable state. |

## Registry Reference

### AI-EXEC-001 - Use autonomous delegated execution for implementer and reviewer agents

**Category:** execution

**Policy:** Delegated implementer and reviewer agents must run autonomously. The lead owns user-facing approvals and clarification.

**Runtime notes:**
- `claude`: Use auto execution mode for implementer and reviewer teammates.
- `codex`: Spawn delegated worker and reviewer agents without user-input-gating patterns.

### AI-EXEC-002 - Reuse implementer agents across waves instead of spawning per task

**Category:** execution

**Policy:** Spawn implementer agents matching peak parallelism, plus one spec reviewer. Reuse idle agents for later-wave tasks.

**Runtime notes:**
- `claude`: Spawn implementers for the first wave only. Reuse idle implementers for later waves via SendMessage with the new task spec.
- `codex`: Spawn workers matching peak parallelism, then reassign idle workers to later tasks instead of creating new workers.

### AI-EXEC-003 - Sync working branch with main before starting implementation

**Category:** execution

**Policy:** Before spawning implementers, fetch origin/main and rebase the working branch onto it. Verify lint and tests pass on the synced branch before proceeding.

**Runtime notes:**
- `claude`: Before spawning implementers: fetch origin/main, rebase onto origin/main, verify lint and tests pass.
- `codex`: Ensure the working branch is rebased onto the latest main and passes lint/tests before starting delegated work.

### AI-EXEC-004 - Restrict implementers to their assigned task only

**Category:** execution

**Policy:** Spawn prompts must explicitly say the implementer should only implement the assigned task, even if other plan tasks are visible.

**Runtime notes:**
- `claude`: Append to every implementer spawn prompt: Only implement your assigned task. Do not work on tasks assigned to other agents.
- `codex`: Include explicit scope restriction in each worker prompt limiting it to the single assigned task.

### AI-PLAN-001 - Pin exact shared type shapes in the plan

**Category:** planning

**Policy:** If parallel or sequential tasks share a type boundary, the plan must pin exact definitions, payloads, and method signatures consumed downstream.

**Runtime notes:**
- `claude`: Include exact definitions, payloads, and downstream-consumed methods for shared types before teammates start.
- `codex`: Include exact type shapes in the plan and pass them into worker prompts unchanged.

### AI-PLAN-002 - Assign module scaffolding to the earliest task that needs compilation

**Category:** planning

**Policy:** Assign required module declarations or scaffold files to the earliest task that needs them, or create a dedicated setup task.

**Runtime notes:**
- `claude`: Put scaffold file creation in the first task that needs it or add a setup task.
- `codex`: Include scaffold files in the earliest owner task or make a separate setup task before parallel workers begin.

### AI-PLAN-003 - Plan cross-module visibility explicitly

**Category:** planning

**Policy:** When a type defined in one module must be consumed from another, include required visibility or export changes in the defining task.

**Runtime notes:**
- `claude`: When downstream tasks need a type, assign required visibility changes to the task that defines the type.
- `codex`: Include export ownership in the producer task whenever a downstream task needs that type across module boundaries.

### AI-PLAN-004 - Anticipate warning cascades when removing blanket suppressions

**Category:** planning

**Policy:** If a task removes a broad dead-code or unused suppression, include ownership for likely follow-on warnings or defer the cleanup.

**Runtime notes:**
- `claude`: Include downstream files in ownership when removing blanket suppressions would expose new warnings.
- `codex`: Plan ownership for follow-on warning cleanup before removing broad suppression attributes.

### AI-PLAN-005 - Mark unstable dependency APIs as guidance-only in plans

**Category:** planning

**Policy:** When a plan references alpha, pre-1.0, or unstable dependencies, mark API examples as guidance-only and instruct implementers to verify against actual docs or source.

**Runtime notes:**
- `claude`: For alpha or pre-1.0 dependencies, mark API examples as guidance only and require verification against current docs or source.
- `codex`: Mark API examples for unstable dependencies as guidance and instruct workers to verify against package documentation.

### AI-TEST-001 - Clean every stateful backend in test setup

**Category:** testing

**Policy:** Test setup and teardown must clean every stateful backend used by the app, not only the primary database.

**Runtime notes:**
- `claude`: Plan test infrastructure against every mutable backend the feature uses.
- `codex`: Require test setup to reset all mutable backends touched by the feature under test.

### AI-TEST-002 - Use serial isolation for shared backend integration tests

**Category:** testing

**Policy:** Integration tests that mutate shared backend state must use serial execution controls when the harness shares backend instances.

**Runtime notes:**
- `claude`: Use serial execution controls for integration tests that share mutable backend instances.
- `codex`: Require serial execution controls for shared-backend integration tests that mutate state.

### AI-TEST-003 - Grep all construction call sites when adding connection-gating settings

**Category:** testing

**Policy:** When a task adds a new optional setting that triggers a connection attempt, search all state-construction paths and guard each one.

**Runtime notes:**
- `claude`: When adding connection-gating settings, search all state-construction call sites and guard each fixture.
- `codex`: When adding a config field that gates a connection, search all state-construction paths in tests and guard each one.

### AI-VERIFY-001 - Defer lint until wave end for same-crate parallel work

**Category:** verification

**Policy:** When multiple parallel tasks modify the same compilation unit, worker-level lint may be deferred until the wave completes while formatting and owned tests still run per task.

**Runtime notes:**
- `claude`: Let affected implementers defer lint only when failures are outside ownership, then verify lint after the wave completes.
- `codex`: In parallel same-unit waves, defer lint to the lead after all workers finish if failures are caused by unowned files.

### AI-CLEANUP-001 - Remove temporary dead-code suppressions after downstream consumers exist

**Category:** cleanup

**Policy:** Any temporary dead-code or unused suppression added to unblock an earlier wave must be reviewed and removed after downstream consumers are wired in.

**Runtime notes:**
- `claude`: Add a post-wave cleanup check for temporary dead-code and unused suppressions.
- `codex`: After downstream wiring lands, search for temporary suppression annotations and remove any no longer required.

### AI-STATE-001 - Prefer atomic state transitions over check-then-act flows

**Category:** state

**Policy:** When the system manages shared mutable state, prefer atomic operations that both check and mutate state in one backend round trip.

**Runtime notes:**
- `claude`: Prefer one-step atomic mutations for contended shared state.
- `codex`: Prefer one-step atomic mutations when multiple requests may contend on the same key, row, or flag.
